title: AgentCanon experiment README metadata contract r9
description: Normative r9 metadata-block schema. The JSON object, kind/status predicates, complete Runner evidence payload schemas, typed no-replace publication result and aggregate, closed failure algebra, actual Runner runtime-root binding, dual-root resource-planner contract, and CLI rules are authoritative; Markdown is rationale only.

x_cli.command: python3 tools/ci/check_experiment_readme.py --repo-root ROOT --path README [--kind KIND] [--status STATUS] [--registry REGISTRY] [--run-dir RUN_DIR] [--schema documents/experiment-readme-contract-r9.json] [--require-runtime] [--json]
x_cli.api: ReadmeCheckResult check_readme(readme_path, *, repo_root, kind=None, status=None, registry_path=None, run_dir=None, schema_path=None, require_runtime=false)
x_cli.metadata_inference: Use metadata readme_kind/status only when corresponding CLI argument is omitted; supplied values must match.
x_cli.path_resolution: Resolve --path relative to --repo-root; resolve each relative binding path relative to --run-dir when provided, otherwise relative to the README directory; resolve registry relative to --repo-root.
x_cli.schema: Omitted --schema loads documents/experiment-readme-contract-r9.json. Supplied --schema must resolve exactly to <repo-root>/documents/experiment-readme-contract-r9.json; packet-local validation is not a worker alternate.
x_cli.registry_delegation: Invoke tools/ci/check_experiment_registry.py exactly once when registry_path is supplied or matrix requires registry; propagate structured failure as ERD-015 and do not duplicate registry predicates.
x_cli.json_output: With --json emit exactly one ReadmeCheckResult object with no log lines; without --json emit human findings but preserve the same result object and exit.

exit_meaning.1: semantic finding(s) with all required runtime inputs available
exit_meaning.2: usage, metadata block, schema, path, unknown-key, argument/metadata, or registry-delegation error
exit_meaning.3: required runtime binding unavailable or required digest/equality check cannot be established

runner_publication_failure.caller: run_cli returns 2 from aggregate; main propagates 2; stderr carries aggregate/per-artifact reason codes; no manifest status is fabricated.

x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.TERM.NONE: No runtime terminal certificate is permitted or required.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.TERM.TERMINAL_REQUIRED: A resolved terminal_evidence.json binding is required and its terminal event must agree with the declared status.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.TERM.FAILURE_REQUIRED: A resolved terminal_evidence.json binding is required; it must record the failed terminal event and exit/failure identity.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.TERM.PARTIAL_REQUIRED: A resolved terminal_evidence.json binding is required; it must record the stopping event that produced the partial partition.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.LOG.NONE: No runtime log binding is required.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.LOG.FAILURE_REQUIRED: A resolved logs binding is required and must identify the failed command and terminal event.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.LOG.PARTIAL_REQUIRED: A resolved logs binding is required and must cover the stop event and every partition disposition.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.PARTIAL.FORBIDDEN: partial_evidence.json must not be bound or claimed.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.PARTIAL.PARTITION_REQUIRED: A resolved partial_evidence.json binding is required; completed, failed, in-flight, and unstarted partitions must be explicit and mutually exclusive.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.REPLACEMENT.REQUIRED: A resolved replacement binding and replacement_link evidence are required; the superseded item cannot be accepted without its replacement identity.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.DIRTY.PLANNED: A planned README may bind a dirty or not-yet-materialized source; it must mark the state planned and may use a declared digest.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.DIRTY.RUNTIME_CLEAN_OR_DIGEST: A runtime README must bind source_snapshot as clean, or declare dirty_state=dirty with a matching observed sha256 digest and explicit dirty-source classification; unknown dirty state fails.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.RUNNER.NONE: No Runner terminal status is used for this plan/report-draft predicate.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.RUNNER.COMPLETED_TO_COMPLETE: run_manifest.status=completed maps exactly to README status=complete; no other spelling is accepted.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.RUNNER.FAILED_EXACT: run_manifest.status must be failed and must agree with the failure terminal/log evidence.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.RUNNER.PARTIAL_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED: README status=partial is accepted only with partial_evidence.json; Runner completed/failed alone cannot infer partial.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.RUNNER.SUPERSEDED_WITH_REPLACEMENT: Superseded requires terminal evidence plus the replacement link; Runner status alone cannot establish superseded.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.CLAIM.NO_OBSERVED: Observed result claims are forbidden; planned references may point forward to a future run/result.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.CLAIM.OBSERVED_ALLOWED: Observed claims are permitted only when all matrix-required runtime bindings and evidence agree.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.FORWARD.NONE: No ordinary forward runtime or observed-result references are accepted. A superseded row may still use its separately named replacement pointer predicate.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.FORWARD.PLANNED_OWNER_ORACLE: Forward references may name a planned owner/config/oracle transition only.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.FORWARD.REPLACEMENT_ONLY: Only the required replacement reference may point forward.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.RUNTIME.NOT_REQUIRED: Runtime evidence is not required by this pair.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.RUNTIME.REQUIRED: All matrix-required runtime bindings must resolve and pass equality/digest checks.
x_status_predicate_definitions.R5.REPLACEMENT.FORWARD_REQUIRED: The required replacement binding may point forward to the newer run/result/report that supersedes this README; this exception does not permit ordinary future runtime claims.

x_kind_status_matrix_notes: Every allowed pair has one predicate_id and direct predicate-id/object fields. The matrix is the sole lookup authority; Markdown cannot add or relax a predicate. forward_references describes ordinary future claims; replacement_pointer_direction separately describes the required supersession link.

x_semantic_checker_oracle.evidence_classes: A slot passes only when every matrix-required minimum evidence class for that slot has at least one non-empty, non-placeholder, resolved evidence record in an allowed state; one evidence record may satisfy multiple slot_ids explicitly listed on that record, but no unstated slot is inferred.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.kind_status: Select exactly one matrix row from readme_kind and status. Reject unsupported pairs and any slot state not listed by that row.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.forward_references: Accept only the row's forward_reference_predicate_id for ordinary future claims. Planned/owner/oracle references are allowed only for plan draft/planned and report draft. Superseded rows set forward_references=false; their separately named replacement pointer may point forward, and no other runtime claim may do so.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.multi_slot_satisfaction: Evidence is reusable across its declared slot_ids; every required slot independently needs its complete class set, and duplicate slot IDs or duplicate metadata blocks fail.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.command_equality: The normalized command in command binding, registry entry, entrypoint/config declaration, and run_manifest command must be byte-for-byte equal after JSON string decoding; normalization does not reorder or rewrite command tokens.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.config_equality: config.json and the config/config_source pointers named by README must resolve to the same canonical JSON value as run_manifest.config_digest/config identity; absent required config equality is an error.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.artifact_equality: Every README output identity must occur exactly once in artifact_manifest; path, size, and optional digest agree when present. Extra artifacts are allowed only when artifact_manifest marks them as auxiliary.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.digest_semantics: Missing optional_digest or null means not checked. A declared digest is checked against the resolved file; an observed digest is checked against the runtime-produced digest. Any required equality mismatch is ERD-016.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.dirty_source_semantics: For planned rows, dirty_state may be dirty/unknown with dirty_policy=allowed_planned and planned evidence. For runtime rows, dirty_state=clean passes without a digest; dirty requires dirty_policy=allow_with_digest, an observed matching sha256 digest, and explicit classification; unknown fails with ERD-010.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.terminal_partial_semantics: failed requires terminal and logs; partial requires terminal, logs, and mutually exclusive partition evidence; complete requires terminal for runtime rows and forbids a partial claim; superseded requires terminal plus replacement with replacement_pointer_direction=forward_required.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.status_mapping: Runner completed maps to README complete, Runner failed maps to failed, Runner running maps to running; partial and superseded require their matrix evidence and are never inferred from completed/failed.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.report_draft: report:draft permits planned references only and forbids observed result claims; all runtime bindings become required when the report status changes to complete/failed/partial/superseded.
x_semantic_checker_oracle.run_manifest_evidence: Resolve run_manifest.json:/evidence/terminal_evidence and /evidence/partial_evidence. Each object must contain the exact relative path, schema_version, and present boolean defined by x_run_manifest_evidence_contract; present must equal filesystem existence and the file schema_version must match.

environment_resource_gpu.comparison: declared identity and allocation equality; no matrix dependency

terminal_partial_manifest.comparison: exact relative path, schema version, present boolean, and filesystem existence

x_runner_status_mapping.completed_normalization: Runner status completed is the sole spelling normalized to README status complete; terminal evidence status uses complete.
x_runner_status_mapping.unmapped_rule: No status is inferred from an unrelated status; partial and superseded require explicit lifecycle outcomes and their matrix evidence.
x_runner_status_mapping.manifest_write_point: finalize_run_manifest emits evidence first, binds /evidence, then writes run_manifest.json and artifact_manifest.json
x_runner_status_mapping.exit_code_rule: exit_code==0 -> outcome complete -> run_manifest.status=completed -> README status=complete; exit_code!=0 -> outcome failed with command_exit_nonzero/managed_command; source_config_error -> named missing_source_config/source_config_preflight factory; partial/superseded only explicit factories
x_runner_status_mapping.destination_owner: run_managed_experiment.py:emit_runner_evidence derives destinations from context.paths.result_dir; no caller destination argument

x_terminal_partial_production.resource_planner_role: tools/experiments/execution_resource_plan.py writes result witnesses below result/<run_name>/resource_plan/ and preserves existing resource/certificate writes below separate runtime_root; it never writes canonical sibling files.

producer_failure_results.evidence_materialization_io: RunnerEvidencePublication.outcome=io; no terminal_evidence payload is emitted

x_terminal_partial_production.write_order: For outcome.status=partial, publish partial_evidence.json first, then attempt terminal_evidence.json; update run_manifest.json only after both results are published or idempotent_existing. On terminal collision/io retain admitted partial evidence, return aggregate status=partial_admitted_terminal_failure and process code 2, and do not finalize/update the manifest.
x_terminal_partial_production.status_evidence_equality: run_manifest.status=completed maps to README status=complete and terminal.status=complete; failed maps to failed; partial and superseded are explicit outcomes and are not inferred from exit codes.
x_terminal_partial_production.checker: tools/ci/check_experiment_readme.py via exact run_manifest:/evidence pointers, payload schema, and SHA-256 recomputation
x_terminal_partial_production.focused_reachable_validation: Future focused boundaries cover missing-source-config, partial ordering, planner output-root exclusion, and concurrent same/different payload publication; no implementation test is created by this audit.
x_terminal_partial_production.failure_behavior: If partial publication is collision/io, terminal publication is not attempted; return partial_publication_failure and process code 2. If partial is admitted and terminal fails, retain partial evidence with retained_orphan_marker; never delete admitted evidence.
x_terminal_partial_production.retry_behavior: Retry uses identical canonical bytes and per-artifact no-replace publication; equal admitted bytes become idempotent_existing, no admitted destination is deleted or replaced, and each retry recomputes the aggregate.
x_terminal_partial_production.process_return_code: 2 for either aggregate publication-failure status; successful aggregate returns outcome.exit_code when non-null, otherwise 0.
x_terminal_partial_production.caller_mapping: run_cli returns the aggregate process_return_code; main propagates it; expected collision/io remain typed publication results and are not converted into RunnerEvidenceOutcome.

x_metadata_inference.metadata_block: Exactly one <!-- experiment-readme-contract ... --> block is required.
x_metadata_inference.omission: If omitted, kind/status are inferred only from the metadata object's readme_kind/status fields. Neither is inferred from headings, filename, path, registry, run_manifest, or prose.
x_metadata_inference.partial_omission: If exactly one CLI argument is supplied, infer only the other field from metadata.
x_metadata_inference.mismatch: A supplied --kind or --status must equal metadata; mismatch emits ERD-020.
x_metadata_inference.unresolved: If metadata is absent, duplicated, or lacks either field needed for inference, emit ERD-022 (or ERD-001/ERD-014 for block count) and exit 2.

x_schema_argument_policy.supplied: A supplied --schema must resolve exactly to canonical_path under --repo-root; all alternate paths are rejected with ERD-021 and exit 2.
x_schema_argument_policy.audit_packet: The packet-local JSON has the same basename for immutable audit validation; this does not create an alternate worker CLI schema.

latest_pointer_shape.path_semantics: Absolute string equal to result_dir/README.md, matching existing latest_result/result_manifest path style.

x_result_readme_owner.status_source: Read the exactly-one metadata block from the selected result README; result_readme.status must be one of running|complete|failed|partial|superseded, equal metadata.status, and result_readme.kind must equal result.
x_result_readme_owner.implementation_change: Extend _latest_payload and _latest_markdown to emit /result_readme with absolute README path, kind=result, metadata status, and run_name, while preserving existing latest_result/latest_result_name/result_manifest/summary_json/visual_report_html keys.
x_result_readme_owner.boundary_test: tests/tools/test_update_latest_result.py: assert LATEST.json:/result_readme path, kind, status and existing pointer fields.

x_run_manifest_evidence_contract.path_resolution: Each evidence.path is a relative filename resolved against the parent directory of run_manifest.json; absolute paths, alternate filenames, and symlink targets fail.

producer.source_shape: An in-memory RunnerEvidenceOutcome is the only canonical source; resource-plan files are never copied into canonical siblings.
producer.canonical_bytes: UTF-8 JSON with sort_keys=true, ensure_ascii=true, separators=(comma,colon), followed by exactly one LF; digest is SHA-256 of the complete bytes written to the destination.
producer.canonical_digest_binding: run_manifest.json:/evidence/<name>/sha256 equals SHA-256 of the destination bytes; checker recomputes it. Evidence payload does not self-embed its digest.
producer.atomicity: Use the atomicity_protocol object; no check-then-replace and no symlink fallback.
producer.collision_behavior: Authentication occurs only after atomic os.link returns EEXIST; exact bytes and digest are required for idempotent success, otherwise ERD-027/ERD-028; no destination replacement.
producer.status_equality: The evidence status, finalized run_manifest.status mapping, and bound README status must be equal after Runner completed->complete normalization.
producer.pre_resource_planner: The same producer is called on every terminal path, including source-config preflight, before any resource planner entry; missing source config emits terminal-evidence/v1 with status=failed, failure_code=missing_source_config, failure_stage=source_config_preflight, and no partial file.
provenance_inputs: config path and snapshot status/digest when available
source_snapshot path and git identity when available
resource planner entry/readback only when reached

atomicity_protocol.temp_creation: Open a unique name in the destination directory with os.open(temp_name, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_NOFOLLOW|O_CLOEXEC, 0644, dir_fd=dir_fd); callers own the temp name.
atomicity_protocol.write_and_sync: Write all canonical bytes, fsync the temp file descriptor, and close it before publication.
atomicity_protocol.publication_primitive: Publish with os.link(temp_name, destination_name, src_dir_fd=dir_fd, dst_dir_fd=dir_fd, follow_symlinks=false); link is atomic no-replace and os.replace/rename-over-destination is forbidden.
atomicity_protocol.directory_sync: After successful link and after temp unlink, fsync the destination directory descriptor.
atomicity_protocol.collision_authentication: On FileExistsError, open destination with O_RDONLY|O_NOFOLLOW|O_CLOEXEC using the same directory descriptor; require a regular file, fstat before and after reading, unchanged file identity/size, and exact byte plus SHA-256 equality.
atomicity_protocol.concurrent_outcomes: For equal payloads exactly one link succeeds and all losing callers return idempotent_existing after authentication. For unequal payloads exactly one link succeeds and every loser returns ERD-027 without modifying the winner. Symlink/non-regular or changing destination authentication returns ERD-027 or ERD-028; no caller replaces it.
atomicity_protocol.cleanup_owner: The emit_runner_evidence invocation that created a temp owns unlink cleanup on every failure and after successful link; cleanup failure is ERD-028 and is reported without deleting a destination it did not create.
atomicity_protocol.focused_concurrency_oracle: Two reachable concurrent emit_runner_evidence calls for one destination: equal canonical payloads yield one published and one idempotent_existing with no temp files; unequal payloads yield one published and one ERD-027, winner bytes/digest remain unchanged, and no temp files remain.

consumer.shape: The Runner finalizer calls the producer, binds exact relative path/schema/present/sha256 objects under /evidence, then writes run_manifest.json; the checker follows these pointers.

terminal.sha256: lowercase 64-hex SHA-256 of exact destination bytes

partial.sha256: lowercase 64-hex SHA-256 when present, null when absent

ordering: if outcome.status=partial, call finalize_partial_evidence and publish partial_evidence.json first
attempt terminal_evidence.json only after partial result is published or idempotent_existing
if both admitted, compute destination digests and bind /evidence objects
lifecycle_write_point.preflight: run_cli source_config_error branch calls finalize_run_manifest with failure_code=missing_source_config; finalize_run_manifest emits terminal evidence before the manifest write and before resource planner entry (which never occurs).
lifecycle_write_point.failure_atomicity: For partial_publication_failure or partial_admitted_terminal_failure, do not finalize/update run_manifest.json. Retain any admitted partial evidence; only publisher-owned temp files may be cleaned. Return process code 2.
lifecycle_write_point.partial_aggregate: finalize_partial_evidence publishes partial then terminal and returns RunnerEvidencePublicationAggregate; both per-artifact results, aggregate status, retained marker, retry, cleanup, and process code are authoritative.
lifecycle_write_point.return_shape: finalize_run_manifest returns RunnerEvidencePublication for non-partial outcomes and RunnerEvidencePublicationAggregate for partial outcomes; expected publication failures never raise.

checker_validation: require exact /evidence objects and exact paths/schema versions for the selected finalized status
require present equals filesystem existence and reject symlinks/non-regular files
recompute SHA-256 over exact canonical bytes and compare /evidence/<name>/sha256
parse terminal-evidence/v1 or partial-evidence/v1 and compare status, failure code/stage, partition, and provenance to run_manifest
apply the selected lookup-authoritative kind/status predicate without reproducing resource/GPU certificate logic
x_run_manifest_evidence_contract.availability_rule: A finalized run_manifest contains both evidence objects with exact path/schema/present fields; terminal statuses require terminal present=true, partial status additionally requires partial present=true, and non-partial statuses require partial present=false and sha256=null. A pre-finalization run:running manifest may omit /evidence.

encoding.json: UTF-8 without BOM; JSON strings contain no NUL; canonical bytes use sort_keys=true, ensure_ascii=true, separators=(comma,colon), and exactly one final LF.
encoding.paths: Every provenance path is a UTF-8 JSON string using forward slash, relative to the repository root, with no leading slash, no drive prefix, and no path segment equal to ..; run_manifest_relative_path is separately relative to result_dir and is exactly run_manifest.json.
encoding.normalization: Normalize paths to POSIX separators and remove a leading ./; normalize UTC timestamps to second precision with Z; normalize identifier strings by preserving case and rejecting surrounding whitespace, NUL, CR, and LF; canonicalize arrays in lexicographic order before serialization.
encoding.nullability: Null is permitted only where the field schema below says null; unavailable preflight paths/digests are null, never omitted.

literal_invariants: status=complete => exit_code=0, failure_code=null, failure_stage=null, stop_reason=null, partition=null, replacement_run_name=null
status=failed => failure_code and failure_stage are one of the paired values declared by RunnerEvidenceOutcome; replacement_run_name=null and partition=null
status=partial => failure_code=partial_stop, failure_stage=partial_finalization, stop_reason is non-null, partition is non-null, replacement_run_name=null
status=superseded => failure_code=superseded_replacement, failure_stage=supersession, replacement_run_name is non-null, exit_code=null, stop_reason=null, partition=null
partition.universe is the complete expected case-id set
the four arrays are pairwise disjoint
the union of completed, failed, in_flight, and unstarted equals universe

partition_invariants: universe contains every expected case exactly once
each case_id occurs in exactly one of completed, failed, in_flight, or unstarted
no partition array contains a value outside universe
the four partition arrays are sorted lexicographically
empty partitions are represented as [] rather than null or omission

x_runner_evidence_outcome.representation: frozen typed record/dataclass with a closed constructor algebra; only the named factories below can construct it. failure_code/failure_stage are not caller-supplied tuple fields.

signatures.from_preflight: build_missing_source_config_outcome(context: RunContext, *, exit_code: int, message: str) -> RunnerEvidenceOutcome
signatures.partial: RunnerEvidenceOutcome.partial(context: RunContext, *, stop_reason: Literal[user_interrupt|timeout|resource_planner_failure|command_failure|runner_failure], partition: PartialPartition, exit_code: int|null) -> RunnerEvidenceOutcome
signatures.emit: emit_runner_evidence(context: RunContext, outcome: RunnerEvidenceOutcome) -> RunnerEvidencePublication (typed result; expected publication failures do not raise)
signatures.finalize: finalize_run_manifest(context: RunContext, manifest: dict[str, object], start_monotonic: float, outcome: RunnerEvidenceOutcome, patterns: EvalArtifactPatterns) -> RunnerEvidencePublication | RunnerEvidencePublicationAggregate; outcome.status=partial returns the aggregate.
signatures.from_timeout: build_timeout_outcome(context: RunContext, *, exit_code: int | None, timeout_seconds: float, message: str) -> RunnerEvidenceOutcome
signatures.from_resource_planner_failure: build_resource_planner_failure_outcome(context: RunContext, *, planner_error: ResourcePlannerFailure, resource_plan_path: Path | None) -> RunnerEvidenceOutcome
signatures.finalize_partial: finalize_partial_evidence(context: RunContext, outcome: RunnerEvidenceOutcome, manifest: dict[str, object], start_monotonic: float, patterns: EvalArtifactPatterns) -> RunnerEvidencePublicationAggregate

missing_source_config.construction_site: run_cli source_config_error branch before resource planner

process_exit_nonzero.construction_site: managed-command completion branch after exact nonzero exit

timeout.exit_code: exact process exit code if available, otherwise null
timeout.manifest: finalize_run_manifest binds terminal evidence and writes run_manifest.status=failed only after successful evidence publication

resource_planner_failure.exit_code: planner-provided exit code if available, otherwise null
resource_planner_failure.manifest: finalize_run_manifest binds terminal evidence and writes run_manifest.status=failed only after successful evidence publication; planner runtime side effects remain separately attributed

runner_failure.exit_code: exact known exit code or null
runner_failure.manifest: finalize_run_manifest binds terminal evidence and writes run_manifest.status=failed only if canonical evidence publication succeeds

publication_failure_not_runner_outcome.status: no RunnerEvidenceOutcome; outcome is RunnerEvidencePublication collision or io
publication_failure_not_runner_outcome.provenance: publication result only; no terminal evidence payload and no finalized run_manifest is written
publication_failure_not_runner_outcome.manifest: run_cli returns publication failure through the existing process error channel; it does not fabricate a terminal failure pair

x_runner_evidence_outcome.existing_exit_code_rule: run_cli maps exit_code==0 to the complete factory and nonzero process exit to command_exit_nonzero/managed_command; timeout, planner, and RunnerFailure require their named typed boundary factories. Source-config preflight uses the named preflight factory. Partial and superseded are never inferred from exit codes.
x_runner_evidence_outcome.no_worker_invention: Unknown failure_code/failure_stage pairs, publication failure codes, arbitrary status strings, unsorted partitions, and caller-supplied destination paths are unconstructible and rejected before serialization.

evidence_materialization_collision.exception_policy: expected FileExistsError is caught and encoded; no exception escapes emit_runner_evidence

evidence_materialization_io.exception_policy: expected OSError/ValueError/UnicodeError is caught and encoded; no exception escapes emit_runner_evidence

unexpected_nonpublication_exception.exception_policy: caught only at the Runner boundary after sanitization

publication_type.representation: frozen typed record/dataclass returned by emit_runner_evidence and finalize_run_manifest

content_sha256.format: lowercase 64-hex SHA-256 of attempted canonical bytes; non-null after serialization

existing_sha256.format: lowercase 64-hex digest of authenticated existing regular destination; otherwise null

temp_path.encoding: repo-root-relative POSIX path; null when no temp was created

result_invariants: published: destination did not exist, canonical bytes were linked into place, content_sha256 is non-null, existing_sha256=null, cleanup_status=cleaned
idempotent_existing: authenticated existing regular bytes equal content_sha256, no destination mutation, existing_sha256=content_sha256, cleanup_status=cleaned or not_needed

collision: destination is different, symlink, non-regular, or changed during authentication; destination is not mutated, existing_sha256 is set only when authenticated, cleanup_status=cleaned or cleanup_failed
io: serialization or expected filesystem operation failed; destination is not replaced, errno/reason/exception_type identify the boundary, cleanup_status records temp cleanup

exception_result_mapping.serializer_ValueError_or_UnicodeError: io, reason_code=serializer_error, exception_type=ValueError or UnicodeError, no exception escapes
exception_result_mapping.temp_or_write_or_fsync_or_link_OSError: io except EEXIST; reason_code identifies operation, errno copied, no exception escapes
exception_result_mapping.link_EEXIST: authenticate destination; equal regular bytes -> idempotent_existing, otherwise collision; FileExistsError is encoded, not raised
exception_result_mapping.unexpected_BaseException: not a publication result; propagates to the RunnerFailure boundary and is not silently converted

publication_type.caller_behavior: finalize_run_manifest returns the typed result. published/idempotent_existing permits exact evidence bindings and manifest publication; collision/io returns to run_cli, which writes no finalized run_manifest and returns the existing publication-failure process code. No caller fabricates a RunnerEvidenceOutcome from collision/io.

failed_pair_algebra.construction_site: tools/experiments/run_managed_experiment.py:run_cli source_config_error branch before resource planner entry
failed_pair_algebra.manifest_status: publish terminal_evidence with status=failed, failure_code=missing_source_config, failure_stage=source_config_preflight; bind /evidence and write run_manifest.status=failed; partial absent

failed_pair_algebra.factory: build_runner_evidence_outcome(context, *, exit_code) after the managed process returns
failed_pair_algebra.construction_site: tools/experiments/run_managed_experiment.py:managed-command completion branch for exact nonzero exit
failed_pair_algebra.manifest_status: publish terminal_evidence with status=failed, failure_code=command_exit_nonzero, failure_stage=managed_command; bind /evidence and write run_manifest.status=failed
publish terminal_evidence with status=failed, failure_code=timeout, failure_stage=managed_command; bind /evidence and write run_manifest.status=failed

failed_pair_algebra.construction_site: tools/experiments/run_managed_experiment.py:resource-plan invocation adapter catch after planner entry
failed_pair_algebra.manifest_status: publish terminal_evidence with status=failed, failure_code=resource_planner_failure, failure_stage=resource_planner; bind /evidence and write run_manifest.status=failed; planner runtime-root writes remain attributed to planner

failed_pair_algebra.construction_site: tools/experiments/run_managed_experiment.py:typed RunnerFailure catch at run_cli/finalization boundary for non-publication failures
failed_pair_algebra.manifest_status: publish terminal_evidence with status=failed, failure_code=runner_failure, failure_stage=runner_finalization; bind /evidence and write run_manifest.status=failed only if publication succeeds

x_resource_planner_output_contract.result_dir_input: Runner passes context.paths.result_dir, the selected directory containing run_manifest.json; it is required and resolved before planner entry.
x_resource_planner_output_contract.runtime_root_input: Runner derives a local runtime_root at execute_managed_run from AGENT_CANON_SHARED_RUNTIME_SOURCE, validates the exact shared runtime directory, and passes it explicitly; RunContext is not expanded.

root_derivation.boundary: witness paths must resolve below result_witness_root; resource/certificate persistence paths may resolve below runtime_root; canonical siblings are outside both planner write sets and owned by Runner.

x_resource_planner_output_contract.run_function: run_resource_plan(plan: ResourcePlan, *, result_dir: Path, runtime_root: Path) -> ResourcePlanResult; both roots are validated by derive_resource_plan_roots and no evidence destination argument is accepted.

result_witnesses.allowed: execution_resource_plan.json and planner result witnesses/certificates below this root

runtime_persistence.allowed: existing runtime-root resource and certificate outputs below this root; this is not a README evidence sibling

canonical_evidence.allowed: terminal_evidence.json and partial_evidence.json only through the Runner publisher; planner has no write permission

x_resource_planner_output_contract.canonical_sibling_rule: The planner must never open, link, rename, replace, or emit result_dir/terminal_evidence.json or result_dir/partial_evidence.json. This rule does not forbid its existing writes below runtime_root.
x_resource_planner_output_contract.runner_call: Inside execute_managed_run after frozen_plan construction and before StandardRunner construction: run_resource_plan(frozen_plan, result_dir=context.paths.result_dir, runtime_root=runtime_root)
x_resource_planner_output_contract.focused_assertion: A reachable execute_managed_run test captures the exact planner call and local runtime_root; the planner-root test records result witnesses under result_dir/resource_plan, preserves existing runtime-root resource/certificate writes, and proves canonical siblings remain untouched.

x_failure_algebra.authority: x_runner_evidence_outcome.failure_pairs and this object; Markdown only explains them.
x_failure_algebra.arbitrary_tuple_rule: No public constructor accepts failure_code and failure_stage independently; each allowed pair is produced only by its named factory and every other pair is statically unconstructible.
x_failure_algebra.producer_exception_rule: evidence_materialization_collision/evidence_publication and evidence_materialization_io/evidence_publication are publication-result classifications only; no factory constructs them as RunnerEvidenceOutcome.

x_partial_finalization_aggregate.representation: frozen typed record/dataclass returned by finalize_partial_evidence and by finalize_run_manifest when outcome.status=partial; it contains the independent publication result for each canonical artifact and never replaces or deletes an admitted destination.

success_return_rule.const: outcome.exit_code when it is an integer in 0..255, otherwise 0

stderr.const: typed aggregate status and per-artifact reason codes

outcome_conversion.const: collision/io remain RunnerEvidencePublication results; no RunnerEvidenceOutcome is fabricated

status_predicates.success_published: partial_result.outcome=published and terminal_result.outcome=published; partial_admitted=true; terminal_admitted=true; admitted=true; retained_orphan_marker=null; process_return_code=outcome.exit_code when non-null else 0
status_predicates.success_idempotent_existing: partial_result.outcome=idempotent_existing and terminal_result.outcome=idempotent_existing; both admitted; retained_orphan_marker=null; process_return_code=outcome.exit_code when non-null else 0
status_predicates.success_mixed: one result is published and the other is idempotent_existing; both admitted; retained_orphan_marker=null; process_return_code=outcome.exit_code when non-null else 0
status_predicates.partial_admitted_terminal_failure: partial_result.outcome in [published,idempotent_existing], partial_admitted=true, terminal_result.outcome in [collision,io], terminal_admitted=false, admitted=false; partial evidence is retained and terminal evidence is not admitted; process_return_code=2
status_predicates.partial_publication_failure: partial_result.outcome in [collision,io], partial_admitted=false, terminal_result=null, terminal_admitted=false, admitted=false; terminal is not attempted; process_return_code=2

x_partial_finalization_aggregate.ordering: For outcome.status=partial, publish partial_evidence.json first. Attempt terminal_evidence.json only after partial_result is published or idempotent_existing. Never publish terminal first and never update run_manifest.json between the two attempts.
x_partial_finalization_aggregate.retained_orphan_marker_rule: Required exactly for status=partial_admitted_terminal_failure; null for all success and partial_publication_failure statuses. It is a diagnostic marker in the returned aggregate, not a file that permits deletion or replacement.

fields.partial_retry: If partial evidence is already admitted with equal bytes, return idempotent_existing; never delete or replace it.
fields.terminal_retry: Retry only terminal publication with the same canonical bytes; equal existing bytes return idempotent_existing; differing/symlink/non-regular destination remains collision and is never replaced.
fields.aggregate_recompute: Each retry returns a new aggregate from the two per-artifact results; previous admitted evidence remains valid.

fields.temp_files: Each per-artifact publisher removes only its own unadmitted temp file after success or expected failure.
fields.admitted_destinations: Deletion, replacement, unlink, and rollback of an admitted partial_evidence.json or terminal_evidence.json are forbidden.
fields.cleanup_failure: cleanup_status=cleanup_failed remains in the relevant per-artifact result and aggregate process_return_code=2; no admitted destination is deleted.
fields.orphan_cleanup: The caller may remove only an unadmitted temp path recorded by the publisher; it may not remove the retained partial evidence.

fields.success: Write /evidence/partial_evidence and /evidence/terminal_evidence exact path/schema/present/sha256 bindings, then write the finalized run_manifest.json and artifact_manifest.json.
fields.partial_admitted_terminal_failure: Do not update or finalize run_manifest.json status/evidence pointers; retain the existing running/pre-finalization manifest and return the aggregate.
fields.partial_publication_failure: Do not attempt terminal publication and do not update/finalize run_manifest.json.
fields.ordering: Manifest update occurs only after both per-artifact results are admitted and their digests are verified.

process_return_code.success: outcome.exit_code when it is an integer, otherwise 0
process_return_code.caller: run_cli returns aggregate.process_return_code; main propagates it. It prints the typed publication reason to stderr and does not convert collision/io into a RunnerEvidenceOutcome.

x_partial_finalization_aggregate.focused_reachable_validation: tests/tools/test_run_managed_experiment.py::test_partial_finalization_aggregate_order_failure_retention_retry_and_return_code covers partial published/idempotent then terminal collision/io, partial-first ordering, retained marker, no deletion, no manifest update, retry idempotence, and exact return code 2. It does not duplicate static slot or matrix predicates.

run_context_change.rule: Do not add runtime_root to RunContext; bind it as a local at execute_managed_run and rebind the ResourceRequest with dataclasses.replace.

x_runner_runtime_root_contract.execute_signature: execute_managed_run(context: RunContext, run_config: Mapping[str, object]) -> ExecutionResult

environment_resolution.required_value: The key is absent or its value is exactly /var/lib/agent-canon/runtime; any other value constructs TypedPreflightFailure(runtime_identity_path_mismatch).
validation: resolved path must be an existing directory
environment_resolution.environment_payload: build_run_environment(context) remains the source of the run environment; the runtime-root key is resolved from os.environ at execute_managed_run and is not silently copied into RunContext.

local_binding_order: request = _resource_request_for_managed_run(context, run_config); this keeps the existing environment resolution and request construction.
request = replace(request, runtime_root=runtime_root); no RunContext field is added.
pass request.environment unchanged to NvidiaSMIResourceProbe.discover and the GpuRunRequest.
invoke run_resource_plan at the frozen-plan boundary before StandardRunner.run.
x_runner_runtime_root_contract.planner_call_site: Inside execute_managed_run, after frozen_plan is complete and before build_admitted_environment/StandardRunner construction: planner_result = run_resource_plan(frozen_plan, result_dir=context.paths.result_dir, runtime_root=runtime_root).
x_runner_runtime_root_contract.planner_api: run_resource_plan(plan: ResourcePlan, *, result_dir: Path, runtime_root: Path) -> ResourcePlanResult
x_runner_runtime_root_contract.path_validation: Both inputs are resolved absolute existing directories; result_dir is the selected run directory containing run_manifest.json; runtime_root resolves to /var/lib/agent-canon/runtime, is distinct from result_dir, and is not inside result_dir; result_witness_root=result_dir/resource_plan.
